ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for mascarpone white pizza; closest parent 'pizza food product' (FOODON_00002572) used as ontology anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Pizza) is the closest parent category; no specific code for white pizza or mascarpone pizza exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for mascarpone white pizza as a specific food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One whole mascarpone white pizza, approximately 300 g, as typically served at an Italian restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Composite estimation from: Pizza, cheese (FDC ID 170720), Mascarpone cheese (FDC ID 173420), Prosciutto/Parma ham (FDC ID 167893), Arugula raw (FDC ID 169387), Tomatoes raw (FDC ID 170457)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA or HPB entry exists for mascarpone white pizza. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composition: approximately 55% pizza base/dough (cheese pizza base reference), 20% mascarpone cheese, 15% parma ham, 5% rucola, 5% tomatoes. Values are per 100 g of the assembled dish. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods entries used for each component.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Pizza, white, average
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 50 is based on the analogous value for white pizza (pizza bianca / cheese pizza) reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2021 international GI tables (Atkinson et al., AJCN 2021). Mascarpone white pizza shares a similar refined wheat dough base and fat-rich topping profile to standard white pizza, which attenuates GI relative to plain bread. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (50 × 73.5) / 100 = 36.8, using 300 g serving with 24.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from parma ham, mascarpone cheese, and pizza dough
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its high sodium content of approximately 520 mg per 100 g (approximately 1,560 mg per full serving), which represents a substantial proportion of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from refined wheat pizza base, partially offset by fat and protein content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this pizza in small portions, as the refined wheat dough contributes a moderate glycemic load (GL approximately 36.8 per full serving); the high fat and protein content from mascarpone and parma ham moderates the glycemic response but the overall carbohydrate load per serving remains significant.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat from mascarpone cheese and elevated sodium from cured ham
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this pizza due to its high saturated fat content (approximately 6.4 g per 100 g, approximately 19.2 g per full serving) from mascarpone cheese, combined with high sodium from parma ham, both of which are associated with increased cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mascarpone White Pizza (Pizza Bianca al Mascarpone) is an Italian-style pizza that uses a creamy mascarpone cheese base instead of the traditional tomato sauce, making it a 'white pizza' (pizza bianca). The pizza is topped with thinly sliced parma ham (prosciutto di Parma), fresh rucola (arugula), and cherry tomatoes, finished with a drizzle of olive oil. The pizza base is made from refined wheat dough, baked in a wood-fired or conventional oven until crisp and golden. The mascarpone provides a rich, lightly tangy, and creamy flavour profile, while the parma ham adds saltiness and umami depth. Fresh rucola contributes a peppery bitterness that balances the richness of the cheese, and the tomatoes add acidity and freshness. This dish is popular in Italian restaurants in Singapore, including at Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at approximately 248 kcal per 100 g, with notable saturated fat from the mascarpone (approximately 6.4 g per 100 g) and high sodium from the cured ham (approximately 520 mg per 100 g). The glycemic index is estimated at 50 (low GI), though the glycemic load per full serving is moderate to high due to the large portion of refined wheat dough. Clinically, this dish is of concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease due to its sodium and saturated fat content, and should be consumed in moderation by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its carbohydrate load.
common_user_queries: Is white pizza healthier than red pizza?
How many calories in mascarpone pizza?
Is mascarpone pizza high in sodium?
Is mascarpone pizza suitable for hypertension?
What is the GI of white pizza?

confidence_reason: No single approved primary source (HPB, USDA, ICMR-NIN) contains a direct entry for mascarpone white pizza as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component composition using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods entries for each ingredient. GI is estimated from the analogous white pizza value in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-weighted estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central entries for cheese pizza base, mascarpone cheese, prosciutto/parma ham, arugula, and tomatoes. No direct entry for this composite dish exists in any approved primary source. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entries used for component estimation and cannot be reliably aggregated. 3. GI of 50 is an analogous estimate based on white pizza (pizza bianca) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables; gi_category is 'low' per the Veritas threshold of GI ≤ 55. 4. The description in the input record refers to 'param ham', which is interpreted as 'parma ham' (prosciutto di Parma), a common transliteration variant used in Singapore restaurant menus. 5. This is an Italian cuisine dish served in Singapore; multilingual naming rules for Singaporean dishes do not apply as this is not a Singaporean dish by origin. Italian cuisine does not have a mandatory local-language naming requirement under the Veritas multilingual matrix; romanized Italian name is provided. 6. Serving size of 300 g is an estimate for one whole pizza as typically served at an Italian restaurant; actual restaurant portion may vary.
